---
title: "Python Tip: Hardware-Agnostic pip freeze"
subtitle: "Avoid hardware-specific dependencies in requirements.txt"
author: "Shep Bryan IV"
description: "Generate hardware-agnostic requirements.txt files without NVIDIA CUDA dependencies."
---



<!-- PANEL 1 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

# Python Tip: Hardware-Agnostic `pip freeze`

Working with PyTorch? Avoid hardware-specific dependencies like NVIDIA CUDA from your `requirements.txt`.
Here's a one-liner to make it hardware-agnostic.

:::



<!-- PANEL 2 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

## The Problem

We often use `pip freeze` to generate a `requirements.txt` file, but it includes all installed packages, including hardware-specific ones like NVIDIA CUDA libraries.

This can cause compatibility issues for users without the same hardware setup.

:::{.code-animation}
```bash
pip freeze > requirements.txt
```
:::

:::



<!-- PANEL 3 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

## The Solution

Use this command to generate a `requirements.txt` that includes PyTorch but excludes hardware-dependent packages:

:::{.code-animation}
```bash
pip list --not-required --format=freeze > requirements.txt
```
:::

:::



<!-- PANEL 4 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

## Wrap-Up

Now you can share your requirements without worrying about hardware compatibility issues.

Follow me for more tips.  
Shep Bryan IV

:::
